items.q: How do you describe weather during a past holiday: 'It was sunny'?
items.why: Weather backdrops in past narratives use IMPERFECT. Preterite implies a single bounded event. 'Cuando llegué, hacía sol' = ongoing weather; llegué = completed action. Standard pattern for holiday writing.

items.q: How is 'll' typically pronounced in Spanish?
items.why: Students pronounce 'll' as English double L. In most Spanish dialects, ll is a YOD sound (like English y) or a 'zh' sound (Argentina). Never pronounced as a doubled English L.

items.q: Which verb is used to say 'I am happy today' (temporary state)?
items.why: Students treat ser and estar as identical. SER for permanent traits, identity, time, origin. ESTAR for temporary states, location, ongoing actions. 'Soy contento' would suggest 'I am a happy person', not 'I'm happy right now'.

items.q: Which is the imperfect of 'estudiar' for 'él'?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: estudiaba. “estudia” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: What is the university entrance exam called?
items.why: Correct answer: EvAU. “ESO” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: Up to what age is compulsory schooling in Spain (end of ESO)?
items.why: The real figure is 16 years. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
